purpose: Whole-app CEO truth for feature claims, proof status, blockers, and audit freshness. This registry is deliberately honest: registry presence is not implementation proof.

features.name: Setup, install, and onboarding
features.user_contract: A new user can install memd, run first-use onboarding, and reach a working local proof without hand-editing hidden state.
allowed_claims: Setup/install/onboarding slice has local 25-star implementation proof; external validation remains pending.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim public 25/25, universal one-command install, production onboarding, or external validation until external replay passes.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run feature setup/install/onboarding proof after installer, CLI bootstrap, setup docs, README/START-HERE, update/uninstall scripts, or release changes.

features.name: Docs and product education
features.user_contract: Docs tell users what memd does, what is proven, what is aspirational, and where to start without conflicting claims.
allowed_claims: Registry defines current whole-app truth fields.
Docs/product education has local proof for navigation, claim-to-proof mapping, and honest pending language when the proof script passes.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim docs prove product behavior without linked executable proof.
Do not claim external validation, dogfood readiness, production readiness, or whole-product 25/25 from local docs proof alone.
features.freshness_policy: Update registry and coverage report with every feature claim, proof artifact, or status change.

features.name: Doctor, status, recovery, update, and uninstall
features.user_contract: A user can inspect health, understand failures, recover safely, update, and uninstall without losing data unexpectedly.
proof_artifacts: local temp proof summary emitted by scripts/verify/feature-doctor-status-recovery-update-uninstall-proof.sh
allowed_claims: Doctor/status/update/uninstall recovery slice has local executable proof for current repository surfaces.
Update and uninstall dry-runs state that .memd memory is preserved by default.
Doctor repair can recover generated bundle env files without deleting seeded memory/state sentinel files in the local proof.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim external verification until an independent replay is recorded.
Do not claim destructive reset safety or complete package-manager uninstall coverage until those contracts exist and are proven.
Do not claim all real-world corruptions are recoverable from this local generated-file recovery proof.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run the lifecycle proof after any doctor/status, setup bundle layout, .memd storage, update, uninstall, reset, or recovery behavior change; add external replay before unblocking 25/25.

features.name: Memory capture, lookup, recall, corrections, provenance, and trust
features.user_contract: Users can capture memories, retrieve them accurately, correct them, and inspect provenance/trust boundaries.
proof_commands: bash scripts/memd-cargo-guard.sh check -p memd-client -p memd-core -p memd-schema
allowed_claims: Memory-core substrate surfaces have current local proof coverage across capture, lookup, resume, corrections, provenance, and trust boundaries.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim reliable memory OS, perfect recall, production-grade trust, or external verification from this local proof artifact alone.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run feature-memory-core-proof plus guarded memory cargo check/tests after ingest, store, lookup, resume, correction, provenance, schema, or trust-boundary changes.

features.name: Context compiler and token savings
features.user_contract: memd compiles useful compact context and reports honest token savings without hiding critical facts.
allowed_claims: Local context compiler/token-savings proof has a reproducible fixture ledger with positive saved tokens, quality retention, and budget enforcement.
Historical in-repository V11/V15 compiler/token proof scripts and dated artifacts are cited as supporting local evidence.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim externally verified token savings until an independent/public replay artifact is registered.
Do not publish broad product-wide savings percentages beyond the local fixture and cited historical artifacts.
features.freshness_policy: Refresh measurements after compiler, prompt, tokenizer, benchmark corpus, budget policy, or quality rubric changes; keep external verification pending until independent replay lands.

features.name: Shared research cache and donor repo extraction
features.user_contract: Teams can reuse extracted research context across repos while preserving source attribution and avoiding cross-project contamination.
allowed_claims: The local inspiration/research lane search cache has executable proof for unchanged-file cache hit/miss behavior.
Current local proof shows source attribution fields and rendered file/line/section metadata for inspiration search hits.
Current contamination guardrails are limited to root/query/limit cache keys, file fingerprints, and a fixed inspiration-lane file allowlist.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim production-safe shared research cache, complete RAG cache safety, complete donor repo extraction, or contamination-free/private-data-safe multi-repo sharing from this local proof alone.
Do not claim external verification until an independent replay is recorded.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run proof after changes to inspiration search cache keys, lane file allowlists, RAG integration, donor extraction, cache formats, or multi-repo research workflows.

features.name: Hive and hivemind coordination
features.user_contract: Multiple agents can coordinate through memd with clear ownership, durable state, and replayable handoffs.
allowed_claims: A current static/local proof validates hive coordination contracts and archived ad hoc artifacts for roster, authority, capability, handoff, task ownership, help/review lanes, dev-server conflict receipts, and claim release.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim production hive/hivemind reliability, continuous dogfood, external verification, or cross-agent leakage/private-context sharing from this proof.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run this proof after hive coordination, authority, capability, live-map, handoff, task-board, or receipt changes; rerun the full hive production proof before upgrading beyond partial/ad hoc.

features.name: Competitor and public benchmark replay
features.user_contract: Benchmark comparisons can be replayed from public fixtures with clear baselines and no private-only assumptions.
allowed_claims: Benchmark replay is tracked and requires current run artifacts.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim competitor superiority without current public replay and methodology.
features.freshness_policy: Refresh after benchmark fixture, baseline, competitor version, or scoring changes.

features.name: Dogfood reliability windows
features.user_contract: The team can demonstrate memd working over sustained real use windows, with failures logged and recovery measured.
allowed_claims: Ad-hoc dogfood exists where artifacts are cited.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim sustained reliability windows until dated window artifacts exist.
features.freshness_policy: Close or refresh windows on release, storage, server, installer, or agent workflow changes.

features.name: External replay and auditor proof
features.user_contract: An outside auditor can replay proofs and reach the same status conclusions from public instructions and artifacts.
allowed_claims: External proof is planned and registered.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim externally verified until an independent replay artifact is recorded.
features.freshness_policy: Refresh instructions and artifacts after every verifier, release, or dependency change.

features.name: Product UX surfaces, dashboard, and CLI language
features.user_contract: Users see clear, consistent language in CLI/dashboard surfaces and understand next actions without reading source code.
allowed_claims: UX surfaces are recognized as a first-class feature area.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim polished product UX or dashboard completeness without walkthrough evidence.
features.freshness_policy: Review after every CLI, dashboard, status, docs, or error-message change.

features.name: Network identity, federation, and market layer
features.user_contract: Networked identities and federation/market behavior are explicit, safe, and not confused with local-only memory behavior.
allowed_claims: Network/federation/market layer is a tracked future feature area unless implementation proof is added.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim active federation, market, or network identity guarantees without implementation and proof.
features.freshness_policy: Audit before any public claim or code path involving network identity, federation, billing, or markets.

features.name: Release and claim honesty gates
features.user_contract: Release claims are limited to what registry status and proof artifacts support.
allowed_claims: Registry audit checks required fields and basic honesty constraints.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim 25/25 or production-ready based on registry presence alone.
features.freshness_policy: Run registry audit before release notes, README claim changes, scorecard changes, or public benchmark updates.

features.name: Cross-harness continuity
features.user_contract: Continuity survives across CLI, server, agent, benchmark, and external replay harnesses with equivalent state interpretation.
allowed_claims: Cross-harness continuity is required and has planned proof.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim seamless cross-harness continuity until replay artifacts pass.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run cross-harness replay after state schema, CLI/server protocol, export/import, or harness changes.
